symbols: white lily — purity, chastity, and the saint's devotion to the truth of the Gospel
Dominican black and white habit — the Order of Preachers founded by Dominic
star on the forehead — tradition that a star appeared on Dominic's forehead at baptism, signifying his future as a light of the Church
description: A bust-length portrait of a bearded young man against a dark brown background. He wears the distinctive black and white Dominican habit — a black cloak over a white collar and tunic. A faint halo surrounds his head, and a small star or cross is visible on his forehead. He holds a stem of white lilies in his right hand before his chest. His expression is serene and direct, with large, dark eyes looking slightly to the viewer's right. The painting style is naturalistic with warm, dark tones and soft modeling of the face.
